questions.title: Sum of Numbers
questions.description:
Given a list of numbers (one per line), calculate the sum of all numbers.

For example, if the input is:
1
2
3

The answer would be 6.

questions.title: Count Even Numbers
questions.description:
Given a list of numbers (one per line), count how many of them are even.

For example, if the input is:
1
2
3
4

The answer would be 2 (since 2 and 4 are even).

questions.title: Find Maximum
questions.description:
Given a list of numbers (one per line), find the maximum value.

For example, if the input is:
5
2
9
1

The answer would be 9.
constraints: At least one number will be provided

questions.title: Find Minimum
questions.description:
Given a list of numbers (one per line), find the minimum value.

For example, if the input is:
5
2
9
1

The answer would be 1.
constraints: At least one number will be provided

questions.title: Count Lines
questions.description:
Given a text input with multiple lines, count the total number of lines.

For example, if the input is:
hello
world
test

The answer would be 3.
constraints: Each line contains at least one character
Lines are separated by newline characters

questions.title: Product of Numbers
questions.description:
Given a list of numbers (one per line), calculate the product of all numbers.

For example, if the input is:
2
3
4

The answer would be 24 (2 × 3 × 4 = 24).

questions.title: Count Odd Numbers
questions.description:
Given a list of numbers (one per line), count how many of them are odd.

For example, if the input is:
1
2
3
4
5

The answer would be 3 (since 1, 3, and 5 are odd).

questions.title: Average Value
questions.description:
Given a list of numbers (one per line), calculate the average (mean) value. Round down to the nearest integer.

For example, if the input is:
10
20
30

The answer would be 20.
constraints: At least one number will be provided

questions.title: Digit Sum Total
questions.description:
Given a list of numbers (one per line), calculate the sum of all digits across all numbers. Ignore negative signs.

For example, if the input is:
123
45
-67

The answer would be 1+2+3+4+5+6+7 = 28.
constraints: Ignore negative signs when summing digits

questions.title: Word Frequency
questions.description:
Given a text with multiple words (space-separated on each line), find the word that appears most frequently. If there's a tie, return the lexicographically smallest word.

For example, if the input is:
hello world hello
world hello test

The answer would be 'hello' (appears 3 times).

examples.input:
hello world hello
world hello test

constraints: At least one word will be provided
questions.puzzle_input:
the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
the dog was not that lazy after all
quick quick the fox said to the dog
over and over the lazy dog slept
the fox jumped over the lazy brown dog
